title: Swipr — TikTok-meets-Polymarket session-accuracy betting (stealth app)
html:
Swipr — TikTok-meets-Polymarket session-accuracy betting (stealth app)

Source: 2026-02-09 Google Doc. Brief but dense PRD-style note for a stealth prediction-feed app. Belongs to the stealth-apps cluster alongside Vrtl Bet, Outpace, and IRL Lore.

TL;DR

  • The core mechanic — session-based accuracy betting: users swipe through 10-50 predictions per session and earn tiered payouts on overall hit rate (60% → 1.2x, 70% → 2x, 80% → 5x). It's a game-show skill challenge, not individual bet outcomes. Compare to the peer wager app concept.
  • The feed: yes/no predictions across sports micro-bets, cultural events (influencers, celebrities), political outcomes, AI-generated gambling events (plinko, dice rolls), and social media metrics. Algorithmically curated, Tinder-style preference filters per user.
  • The competitor map: Cheddar (cheddr.xyz) is the closest analog — sweepstakes loophole, A16Z-backed, targets 18-21yo. Past failures (Bookee 2016, Betr) tied themselves to live-sports infra. Yahir's edge: sidestep real-time data costs by using events that resolve over hours/days.
  • The business model trick: charge session entry fees instead of taking house edge per bet. Mix gimmes + coin flips + long shots to engineer a mathematical house edge while users feel they're playing 50/50. Lower infra cost (no streaming data), higher viral surface (share upcoming sessions with friends).
  • Tech stack notes: dual-currency for sweepstakes compliance, event aggregation pipeline (manual + APIs + AI generators), session management, ML feed personalization. Aeropay mentioned as payment rail. "Most expensive expense will be legal, can't fuck this up."
  • Open questions captured: React Native vs Swift, real legal cost breakdown, optimal backend stack, what does the viral session-swipe mechanism actually look/feel like.

Pattern observed

This is the most legally-fraught of the stealth apps cluster — Yahir explicitly flags legal as the dominant cost. Same "skill not chance" framing that Outpace's challenge entry fees use. The mixed-difficulty payout engineering is the clever bit — gives the user perceived control while preserving operator margin. Cheddar reference shows Yahir is benchmarking against actual VC-backed competition.

Note the cross-pollination with IRL Lore: both apps lean on session/feed engagement loops + paywall-on-reveal. Yahir's product instinct is converging on "addictive daily ritual + premium reveal."

Key quotes

  • "You're creating an entirely new prediction marketplace where users can customize their feed preferences (sports-only, sports + culture, everything, etc.) similar to how Tinder users set dating preferences."
  • "Most expensive expense will be legal, can't fuck this up."

Open questions

  • Has Swipr advanced past concept doc? (The "dfdfd" placeholder mid-doc suggests early-draft state.)
  • Does Yahir actually want to build this or is it filed under "ideas to revisit"? (Belongs in inventory.)
  • The cross-addiction thesis — sports bettor + crypto degen + political junkie + casino gambler in one feed — is it actually realistic given divergent regulatory regimes by category?

Related

  • vrtl bet
  • outpace
  • irl lore
  • cheddar cheddr xyz
  • aeropay
  • swiftui
